feed.title: Mina Kwon
feed.subtitle: This is a personal webpage of Mina Kwon

entry.title: E-poster at SOBP 2020
entry.content:
2020 Society of Biological Psychiatry (SOBP) Annual Meeting might have been my first presentation abroad, but it has been canceled due to the COVID-19.
If you have the SOBP membership, please checkout the ePoster Gallery!
The title is “Neural Correlates of Goal-Directed Control in Video Game Addiction and Alcohol Use Disorder.”
entry.summary: 2020 Society of Biological Psychiatry (SOBP) Annual Meeting might have been my first presentation abroad, but it has been canceled due to the COVID-19. If you have the SOBP membership, please checkout the ePoster Gallery! The title is “Neural Correlates of Goal-Directed Control in Video Game Addiction and Alcohol Use Disorder.”

entry.title: Won the SNU Graduate Scholarship for Basic Science Research

link.title: Won the SNU Graduate Scholarship for Basic Science Research

entry.content:
I won my first scholarship as a graduate student!
Due to COVID-19, ceremony wasn’t held this year.
All, please stay safe & healthy!
entry.summary: I won my first scholarship as a graduate student! Due to COVID-19, ceremony wasn’t held this year. All, please stay safe & healthy!

entry.title: My work presented at sfN poster session!

link.title: My work presented at sfN poster session!

entry.content:
My first project using the Adolescent Brain and Cognitive Development (ABCD) study was presented at poster session in Society for Neuroscience 2019 meeting (Chicago, IL), by the second author Hyeonjin Kim.

The title is “Identifying behavioral and neural predictors of caffeinated soda intake in childhood using a large database from the ABCD study.”
entry.summary: My first project using the Adolescent Brain and Cognitive Development (ABCD) study was presented at poster session in Society for Neuroscience 2019 meeting (Chicago, IL), by the second author Hyeonjin Kim.

entry.title: Graduated from Seoul National University :)
entry.content:
From 2015 to 2019, I studied Psychology and Brain-Mind-Behavior in Seoul National University.
I’m becoming a graduate student in Computational Clinical Science Laboratory, studying clinical psychology.
I look forward to doing wonderful research with my PI Dr. Woo-Young Ahn and my colleagues! ;-)
entry.summary: From 2015 to 2019, I studied Psychology and Brain-Mind-Behavior in Seoul National University. I’m becoming a graduate student in Computational Clinical Science Laboratory, studying clinical psychology. I look forward to doing wonderful research with my PI Dr. Woo-Young Ahn and my colleagues! ;-)

entry.title: Presentation at the 2019 Annual Meeting of the Korean Cognitive Science Society

link.title: Presentation at the 2019 Annual Meeting of the Korean Cognitive Science Society

entry.content:
This was my first time presenting at a conference!
Title of my presentation was “Identifying behavioral and neural predictors of caffeinated soda intake in childhood using a large database from the ABCD study.”
This study was developed from my previous work that I presented at the BMB symposium. ;-)
entry.summary: This was my first time presenting at a conference! Title of my presentation was “Identifying behavioral and neural predictors of caffeinated soda intake in childhood using a large database from the ABCD study.” This study was developed from my previous work that I presented at the BMB symposium. ;-)

entry.title: Received SNU undergraduate research grant!
entry.content: I received an undergraduate research grant at Seoul National University! I’m going to study smoker’s behavior with eye-movement data :)
entry.summary: I received an undergraduate research grant at Seoul National University! I’m going to study smoker’s behavior with eye-movement data :)

entry.title: Presentation at the Brain-Mind-Behavior symposium
entry.content:
I completed my independent research projects in Fall 2018.
Using a large database from the ABCD study, I focused on a relationship between children’s caffeinated soda intake and neuro-cognitive deficits.
I learned a lot about using python, analyzing data, making poster, preparing presentations, etc.
Plus, I also won a poster award! :-)
entry.summary: I completed my independent research projects in Fall 2018. Using a large database from the ABCD study, I focused on a relationship between children’s caffeinated soda intake and neuro-cognitive deficits. I learned a lot about using python, analyzing data, making poster, preparing presentations, etc. Plus, I also won a poster award! :-)
